Now, what are the advantages of three-D laparoscopy? Now, one thing is depth perception. Many people ask me, three-D laparoscopy or four-K? Okay, four-K can give you very detailed view, but three-D laparoscopy, you get this depth perception, which is wonderful when you're operating. Now, it is also, of course, high resolution. Of course, you can't compare this highest resolution to four K, but it's still very high resolution. and you get less tired, you don't realize it. Many people who do two-D laparoscopy tell me that I can do the surgery by two-D laparoscopy. But when you operate for a long time and your brain is actually trying to convert a three-D view into a two-D view to operate, you get tired, you don't realize it.
Only when you do three-D laparoscopy you realize how less tiring it is when you're doing three-D laparoscopy.
But there are some disadvantages of three-D laparoscopy. Now, the first disadvantage is the camera pod need to be at higher level in the umbilicus.

As you can see here, most of the time I'm operating at a pod higher up and not in the umbilicus. The reason is because the three-D laparoscope has got a zoom view. We call it a plus one. as opposed to a zero point eight when you can do with a two D laparoscopy. So you have to be further away.

And this is one of the biggest disadvantage of a three D laparoscopy, I think. The other one is only ten millimeter telescope is available. As you can see, this is a ten millimeter telescope. I'm looking forward to them bringing in the eight millimeters and five millimeters in the future. Another thing is

This telescope comes with either zero or thirty degrees. I'm a big fan of thirty degree telescopes and even today I use thirty degree telescopes because I have the ability to look at the site. For three-D, you cannot do that. You cannot look at the site. You only can look vertically down or vertically up.
You cannot turn to the side and I hope this is another improvement that in future we will get. So these are the three small disadvantages of three-D laparoscopy.
